review_title: Not Perfect but it Helps
author: Katie P.
pros: The customization of job board integration.
cons: How long it takes to parse a resume and how LinkedIn is consistently giving me issues with integrating with Daxtra. It is also not an east software to understand at first; i.e. how to use the resume match to search for candidates effectively.

review_title: Daxtra is a great tool as long as you understand its capabilities and limitations
description: The people at Daxtra are wonderful to work with. We love rep team. They have really gone above and beyond to support us.
author: Carla S.
pros: quickly searches across your company CRM/database and job boards that you have licenses with. works best with careerbuilder, dice and linkedin Recruiter
cons: does not work with indeed because indeed does not share contact information (NOT the fault of Daxtra, indeed is protective of their candidates). ALSO they do NOT have a relationship with ZipRecruiter. No fault to our rep but it sounds like Zip Recruiter won't work with Daxtra. Perhaps Daxtra could find ways to incentive Zip Recruiter to partner.

review_title: Serious power but overly engineered
description: Daxtra is one of the better tools when used properly, but it's very difficult to get users to adopt due to it's extreme robustness.
author: Zach S.
pros: This tool has every bell, whistle, and view you'd want as a sourcer/recruiter to find candidates.
cons: The interface is so cluttered and unintuitive that it's difficult to gain user adoption, especially with more senior recruiters. They see it as hard work just to use the tool rather than realize it's full potential. Daunting once a search is run. It needs to be simplified in view but powerful behind the scenes.

review_title: Love this tool!
description: DaXtra saves time by searching multiple sources at one time!
author: Kat L.
pros: We've made a couple of major placements finding the purple squirrel candidate with DaXtra search.
cons: There are a LOT of bells and whistles and it can be confusing for our recruiters who aren't entirely technical.

review_title: Loving this tool for my desk!
description: I think its a lot to learn at first but once you get used to it it helps to stream line processes a lot!
author: Taylor F.
pros: I love using this tool for my full cycle recruiting desk! We have been using this for a few months now and I feel like it really helps process and speed to submittal!
cons: I do not notice a lot of cons right now, just a learning curve but liking it so far!

review_title: Early issues = Recruiter skepticism
description: Your people are exceptional: [SENSITIVE CONTENT] have been amazing in implementation and use/troubleshooting. Very knowledgeable, friendly, responsive.The suite of products simply have not added value to our recruiting efforts to support the time, energy and distraction of the implementation and usage. Recruiting has not embraced the tool because they lost trust early in the launch process.
author: Laura H.
pros: The concept of the software is fantastic.
cons: We are not realizing the reality of the concept. Regular issues and hiccups. Challenges with our BH configurations specific to LinkedIn and got that problem solved after a lot of energy and time. Other hiccups with our BH configuration. Match is not returning the quality results we'd anticipated.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Integrates with Bullhorn Well
author: Shannon A.
pros: I like that this product integrates with our Bullhorn database. It makes it easy to parse resumes into our system and can scan our system for duplicates before uploading.
cons: Dice/Monster both have searching features not available with Daxtra. I.e.- searching on specific work authorizations, etc.

review_title: Use of Daxtra for resume parsing and formatting
description: Our goal with Daxtra is to automate the parsing of a resume to populate onto our candidates and match them to jobs in our system. so far, it has done it but it required and continues to require configuration changes and testing to make sure it is reliable. i think there is still some work on how daxtra is picking up the job titles and identifying which job title is most current. the support from daxtra has been helpful. however, in our most recent experience, we had to ask support for updates after several days of not receiving any. also, the version updates are difficult to test. the descriptions are not enough. would like to know what the new version changes would expect to pick up now in a resume. we had a lengthy email chain on this for the last release.
author: Stan C.
pros: Once it is up and going, there is very little to none maintenance. Support has been responsive.
cons: The difficulty in understanding what happens under the hood. Why a particular item was parsed. The testing is tedious and difficult sometimes.

review_title: Great idea that needs additional development
description: That said, the suggestions Daxtra makes for misspelling and synonyms is useful, especially because they can be deleted or others added.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The idea to combine searching on various platforms is great. It could be a real time saver.
cons: The searches don't really work that well, and because each search platform has different search inputs, one search entry for different platforms does not work that well.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Using Daxtra
description: Daxtra has saved us an unlimited number of hours sourcing through applicant resumes and the uploading of resumes into our ATS.
author: Debra Q.
pros: Resumes that are not significant to our job postings are filtered out for review before they are uploaded into our ATS system. Daxtra not only creates new candidate profiles for us, but also updates candidates that are already in our system. Daxtra also creates tasks to the correct recruiter so they are aware a new candidate has been created or updated.
cons: The Daxtra dashboard it a little cumbersome in trying to correct Failed to Loads and making changes. I do not like the difficulty of adding new certifications and key words to Daxtra.

review_title: Still working out the kinks
description: Our users are about 50/50 on whether it's beneficial or not. We're still in the process of working out the kinks and coming up with a workflow that works for most people.
author: Katie B.
pros: I like that we can search multiple resume databases at once. Several of our recruiters also like the Watchdog and find it helpful.
cons: There seem to be some hiccups between Daxtra and both Careerbuilder and LinkedIn that make it easier for us to search CB and LI independently, which defeats the purpose or having Daxtra. The resume styler doesn't perform well for us either. After working with the help desk, we've determined it's better for us to just manually format our resumes.

review_title: Great product backed by a terrific team
description: Great experience.
author: Patrick D.
pros: It delivers on what is advertises... The parser has massively reduced the amount of duplicates in our system and has kept records with bad data out. Search has increased the # of resumes getting added to our system by 500% and consolidating the search interface has increased productivity. Their Machine Learning has also shortened the necessary ramp up time for new recruiters to get comfortable with technical sourcing.
cons: There is a LOT to this product... you really have to lay the groundwork with your teams internally to optimize their use of it. You cannot recruit out of Daxtra, its best used for sourcing, building lists and then leveraging your CRM for actually reaching out to those potential matches.

review_title: Not the Greatest Job Board Integrator
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: The price point is the only benefit to this tool. The price for Daxtra compared to competitors (Broadbean/TextKernel) is less expensive.
cons: Integration issues, Customer Service, User Interface are the top three. We are consistently resolving integration issue with Resumes not loading/failing into our ATS. The Customer service always seems to tell us that nothing can be done to resolve the issues we are seeing. The user interface not comparable to its competitors as well.

review_title: Most Sophisticated Search Tool Available!
description: Daxtra is the most sophisticated search tool on the market. Make the tool work for you, all the capabilities are there.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Daxtra Search will search your database and other job boards in one stop. The semantic search abilities are a strong assist to recruiting productivity, speed, and quality. In comparison to our previous ATS search features, Daxtra Search is able to render quality search results which the ATS could not. The sales process and onboarding of the tool went smoothly by having an ATS and Technology expert on each side of the project.
cons: There is no least liked feature. A challenge may be the overall adoption from all staff members which is dependent on the staff's technical abilities and willingness to accept change. A dedicated training resource is provided by Daxtra to help facilitate.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Daxtra will aggregate your job board subscriptions
description: It will integrate with many applicant tracking systems and could be the most cost effective option for some businesses.
author: Jake B.
pros: Daxtra does allow you to add in most job boards you would ever use to do an aggregated search. It contains all of the main features like saved searches to help you passively and more efficiently actively recruit.
cons: It is not the best looking product and not the easiest to use as a user or an admin.

review_title: Very good integrated search for Bullhorn users
description: overall very positive - good product.
author: Patrick B.
pros: Integrated into Bullhorn and the ability to search multiple boards with one search string. Also the watchdog (saved search) feature is great to find the recent / new candidate.
cons: Watchdog email for saved searches should have hyper links back to the potential candidates.

review_title: The devil is in the details.
description: Oversold and pretty much blah.
author: Steve G.
pros: It automatically loads candidates who apply into our database and when it is parsed correctly, it keeps our database clean.
cons: The implementation takes several months. The parsing tool needs to be constantly monitored. The tool is touted as being a foolproof system and there are many gaffs and nuances (i.e. search terms,
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Great product
description: Daxtra has been a game changer for me and my firm. Streamlined candidate searches, complete integration with our ATS, and overwhelming support and guidance from the Daxtra team has made our interaction with their product a true win!!!
author: Stu W.
pros: I like Daxtra's ability to combine searches from different engines and make for one stop shopping with my job board subscriptions.
cons: It's not free. Also, does not work well with LinkedIn

review_title: The Delta Companies
description: Overall experience with Daxtra has been great. Anytime I've had questions or concerns, my account manager has always been on top of it.
author: Marissa L.
pros: Daxtra has significantly improved the automation process in terms of updating new and current providers within our database.
cons: Occasionally, connection with our API gets lost causing internal delays. Additionally, there are many hiccups with the missing info and failed to process features.

review_title: Daxtra search and parsing tools work well but may be a duplicate of other tools you have
description: Daxtra was useful for automatically parsing candidate resumes and job applications into our database application (Bullhorn) and for searching our database to find qualified candidates for jobs we were trying to fill on behalf of our clients.
author: Sheena S.
pros: The Daxtra search tool for Bullhorn ATS/CRM is comprehensive and returns good results for most searches. The resume parsing tool also captures data accurately most of the time, even though the back end interface is a bit clunky/outdated feeling.
cons: Changes to the Daxtra resume parsing tool meant job applications were not being assigned to the correct job record in the Bullhorn database. The system (last used in October 2018) required data to be transmitted to Daxtra in a fairly specific way for it to parse accurately, which meant a fair bit of manual configuration on our end.

review_title: Great Product, but it has some limitations
author: Emanuela R.
pros: - Able to search multiple job boards - The magnet tool is very helpful!
cons: - Daxtra doesn't search across ZipRecruiter, this would be very helpful, as most of our employees use ZipRecruiter. - You are not able to mass text and email people directly with Daxtra, like you can with other job boards.

review_title: O.K. for Sourcing mult. Job Boards
description: It's ok if you have room in your budget.
author: Catherine H.
pros: Daxtra performs searches for multiple job boards.
cons: Sometimes Daxtra pulls different results than what's on the actual job board. You also have to have a license to each job board in order to have the capability to search all boards.

review_title: Great product
description: I like the ability to keep our recruiters inside of Bull Horn full time and not have them navigating through multiple websites and potentially have data slip through the cracks. I also like the "mass upload" feature that allows you to scrape job board resumes into your ATS based a particular search parameters.
author: Chris W.
pros: Easy to implement Easy to drive user adoption
cons: Expensive software Lack of LinkedIn integration

review_title: New to Daxtra, But loving it So far!
description: We have a good partner in Daxtra, who worked with us in getting the product setup, provided good training and have assisted us with quick responses as we have run into any issues.
author: Ben W.
pros: Daxtra worked with us to get their product easily integrated with our CRM. They were great to work with and worked with us to ensure our customization needs were met, and that we had the product working in our favor. The features meet our users needs and allow for greater efficiencies with Candidates.
cons: During integration we did run into a few hiccups with custom items not aligning correctly, and ensuring the job boards connected up has been slightly troublesome, but we have had great help in connecting with Daxtra to resolve them.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Thankful for your Continued Support
description: Good experience with DaXtra and looking for the updates to make things better.
author: Mary C H.
pros: Software at first was not as helpful as we first anticipated. We have worked with [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] to help educate and update some of concerns with the software. With the new updates we are in process of working with the upgrades and are in the process of working through them we will have more information in about 2 weeks.
cons: One issue that we currently do not like that unless we add a candidate into Bullhorn through Magnet, when searching with Bullhorn some candidates will not pull in our searches.
